examples.title: Algorithm: Sparse Matrix Insertion and Display
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating how to insert non-zero elements into a linked-list based sparse matrix and display operations
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CreateSparseMatrix(rows, cols)
INPUT: Number of rows and columns
OUTPUT: Empty sparse matrix structure
BEGIN
 Allocate matrix structure
 SET matrix.rows = rows
 SET matrix.cols = cols
 SET matrix.numNonZero = 0
 Allocate row_head array of size rows (all NULL)
 Allocate col_head array of size cols (all NULL)
 RETURN matrix
END

ALGORITHM InsertElement(matrix, row, col, value)
INPUT: matrix, position (row, col), non-zero value
OUTPUT: Updated sparse matrix
BEGIN
 IF value == 0 THEN RETURN (do not store zeros)
 IF row < 0 OR row >= matrix.rows OR col < 0 OR col >= matrix.cols THEN RETURN
 
 CREATE newNode with row, col, value, next_row=NULL, next_col=NULL
 
 // Insert into row list (sorted by column)
 IF matrix.row_head[row] == NULL OR matrix.row_head[row].col > col THEN
 SET newNode.next_col = matrix.row_head[row]
 SET matrix.row_head[row] = newNode
 ELSE
 SET temp = matrix.row_head[row]
 WHILE temp.next_col != NULL AND temp.next_col.col < col DO
 SET temp = temp.next_col
 END WHILE
 SET newNode.next_col = temp.next_col
 SET temp.next_col = newNode
 END IF
 
 // Insert into column list (sorted by row)
 IF matrix.col_head[col] == NULL OR matrix.col_head[col].row > row THEN
 SET newNode.next_row = matrix.col_head[col]
 SET matrix.col_head[col] = newNode
 ELSE
 SET temp = matrix.col_head[col]
 WHILE temp.next_row != NULL AND temp.next_row.row < row DO
 SET temp = temp.next_row
 END WHILE
 SET newNode.next_row = temp.next_row
 SET temp.next_row = newNode
 END IF
 
 INCREMENT matrix.numNonZero
END

ALGORITHM DisplayMatrix(matrix)
INPUT: Sparse matrix
OUTPUT: Dense matrix representation printed
BEGIN
 FOR i = 0 TO matrix.rows - 1 DO
 SET curr = matrix.row_head[i]
 FOR j = 0 TO matrix.cols - 1 DO
 IF curr != NULL AND curr.col == j THEN
 PRINT curr.value
 SET curr = curr.next_col
 ELSE
 PRINT 0
 END IF
 END FOR
 PRINT newline
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the core algorithms for creating a sparse matrix using linked lists, inserting elements (maintaining sorted order in both row and column lists), and displaying the matrix in dense form by traversing row lists.

examples.title: Python: Sparse Matrix Using Linked Lists
examples.description: Complete Python implementation of sparse matrix with linked list representation, insertion, display, and transpose operations
examples.code:
class SparseNode:
 """Node for storing a non-zero element in sparse matrix"""
 def __init__(self, row, col, value):
 self.row = row
 self.col = col
 self.value = value
 self.next_row = None # next node in same column (downward)
 self.next_col = None # next node in same row (rightward)


class SparseMatrix:
 """Sparse Matrix using multilist (cross-linked) representation"""
 
 def __init__(self, rows, cols):
 self.rows = rows
 self.cols = cols
 self.num_non_zero = 0
 self.row_head = [None] * rows # row header pointers
 self.col_head = [None] * cols # column header pointers
 
 def insert(self, row, col, value):
 """Insert a non-zero element into the sparse matrix"""
 if value == 0:
 return # do not store zeros
 if row < 0 or row >= self.rows or col < 0 or col >= self.cols:
 return # invalid position
 
 # Create new node
 node = SparseNode(row, col, value)
 
 # Insert into row list (sorted by column)
 if self.row_head[row] is None or self.row_head[row].col > col:
 node.next_col = self.row_head[row]
 self.row_head[row] = node
 else:
 temp = self.row_head[row]
 while temp.next_col is not None and temp.next_col.col < col:
 temp = temp.next_col
 node.next_col = temp.next_col
 temp.next_col = node
 
 # Insert into column list (sorted by row)
 if self.col_head[col] is None or self.col_head[col].row > row:
 node.next_row = self.col_head[col]
 self.col_head[col] = node
 else:
 temp = self.col_head[col]
 while temp.next_row is not None and temp.next_row.row < row:
 temp = temp.next_row
 node.next_row = temp.next_row
 temp.next_row = node
 
 self.num_non_zero += 1
 
 def display_dense(self):
 """Display the sparse matrix in dense (full) form"""
 print(f"Sparse Matrix ({self.rows}x{self.cols}) with {self.num_non_zero} non-zero elements:")
 for i in range(self.rows):
 curr = self.row_head[i]
 row_values = []
 for j in range(self.cols):
 if curr is not None and curr.col == j:
 row_values.append(f"{curr.value:4d}")
 curr = curr.next_col
 else:
 row_values.append(f"{'0':4d}")
 print(''.join(row_values))
 
 def display_triplets(self):
 """Display non-zero elements in triplet form (row, col, value)"""
 print("Row Col Value")
 print("--- --- -----")
 for i in range(self.rows):
 curr = self.row_head[i]
 while curr is not None:
 print(f"{curr.row:3d} {curr.col:3d} {curr.value:5d}")
 curr = curr.next_col
 
 def transpose(self):
 """Transpose the sparse matrix by swapping row/col indices"""
 trans = SparseMatrix(self.cols, self.rows)
 
 # Traverse column-wise and insert with swapped indices
 for j in range(self.cols):
 curr = self.col_head[j]
 while curr is not None:
 trans.insert(curr.col, curr.row, curr.value)
 curr = curr.next_row
 
 return trans
 
 def add(self, other):
 """Add two sparse matrices (must have same dimensions)"""
 if self.rows != other.rows or self.cols != other.cols:
 raise ValueError("Matrices must have same dimensions")
 
 result = SparseMatrix(self.rows, self.cols)
 
 # Process each row
 for i in range(self.rows):
 p1 = self.row_head[i]
 p2 = other.row_head[i]
 
 while p1 is not None and p2 is not None:
 if p1.col < p2.col:
 result.insert(i, p1.col, p1.value)
 p1 = p1.next_col
 elif p1.col > p2.col:
 result.insert(i, p2.col, p2.value)
 p2 = p2.next_col
 else:
 # Same column - add values
 if p1.value + p2.value != 0:
 result.insert(i, p1.col, p1.value + p2.value)
 p1 = p1.next_col
 p2 = p2.next_col
 
 # Copy remaining elements
 while p1 is not None:
 result.insert(i, p1.col, p1.value)
 p1 = p1.next_col
 while p2 is not None:
 result.insert(i, p2.col, p2.value)
 p2 = p2.next_col
 
 return result


# Demo usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
 # Create sparse matrix from example
 mat = SparseMatrix(4, 5)
 
 # Insert non-zero elements
 mat.insert(0, 2, 3)
 mat.insert(0, 4, 4)
 mat.insert(1, 2, 5)
 mat.insert(1, 3, 7)
 mat.insert(3, 1, 2)
 mat.insert(3, 2, 6)
 
 print("=== Original Matrix (Dense Form) ===")
 mat.display_dense()
 
 print("\n=== Non-zero Elements (Triplet Form) ===")
 mat.display_triplets()
 
 print("\n=== Transpose Matrix ===")
 trans = mat.transpose()
 trans.display_dense()
 
 # Create another matrix for addition demo
 mat2 = SparseMatrix(4, 5)
 mat2.insert(0, 2, 1)
 mat2.insert(1, 3, -2)
 mat2.insert(2, 4, 3)
 
 print("\n=== Matrix Addition Result ===")
 result = mat.add(mat2)
 result.display_dense()
examples.explanation: Complete Python implementation showing the multilist representation with five fields per node (row, col, value, next_row, next_col). Includes insertion maintaining sorted order, dense display, triplet display, transpose by rebuilding with swapped indices, and matrix addition by merging row lists. Time complexities: Insert O(r+c), Transpose O(nonzero), Add O(nonzero_A + nonzero_B).
examples.timeComplexity: Insert: O(r+c), Display: O(rows*cols), Transpose: O(nonzero), Add: O(nonzero_A + nonzero_B)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(nonzero) for storage plus O(rows + cols) for headers

examples.title: C: Sparse Matrix Using Linked Lists
examples.description: Complete C implementation of sparse matrix with linked list representation, including insert, display, transpose and addition operations
examples.explanation: Complete C implementation of sparse matrix using multilist representation. The node structure has five fields as specified in the theory: row, col, value, next_row, next_col. Header arrays (row_head and col_head) anchor the row and column linked lists. Operations include insert maintaining sorted order, display in dense and triplet forms, transpose by rebuilding with swapped indices, and addition by merging row lists. Demonstrated with the 4x5 example from the topic.
examples.timeComplexity: Insert: O(r+c), Display: O(rows*cols), Transpose: O(nonzero), Add: O(nonzero_A + nonzero_B)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(nonzero) for storage plus O(rows + cols) for headers
